content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 29, 1989


Denise Stadelbacher, Treasurer
Jonathan Kinsman for Mayor Campaign
Committee
4272-1/2 University Avenue
Riverside, CA 92501

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-455

Dear Ms. Stadelbacher:

You have requested confirmation of telephone assistance I
provided to you on behalf of Jonathan Kinsman concerning his
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

Your letter, dated July 26, 1989, correctly summarizes my
advice that Mr. Kinsman may retain a contribution received prior
to filing a Candidate Intention (Form 501) as required by Section
85200. There is no requirement in the Act or Commission
regulations for returning contributions which are not in
compliance with Section 85200. For your convenience, a copy of
your letter is attached.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662 if you
have additional questions concerning this matter.

sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

Attachment


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

FPPC
JUL 28 11 44 AM '89

July 26, 1989


Dear Ms. Kvasager:

I would greatly appreciate written confirmation of the decision reached during
our phone conversation held on July 25, 1989 at 4:45 PM. This allowed for
Jonathan Kinsman, candidate for Mayor of the City of Riverside, to retain a check
he received on May 26, 1989. This was prior to the filing of FPPC Form 501
and 502 on June 1, 1989. The check in question was deposited on the same date
that the forms were filed. Thank you for giving this matter prompt consideration.


Sincerely,

Denise Stadelbacher

Denise Stadelbacher, Treasurer
Jonathan Kinsman for Mayor Campaign
Committee, ID# 890962


4272 ½ Universiti Ave
Riverside 92501

7/4-275-9168

894-4553

FPPC
JUL 28 11:44 AM '89

July 26, 1989


Dear Ms. Kvasager:

I would greatly appreciate written confirmation of the decision reached during
our phone conversation held on July 25, 1989 at 4:45 PM. This allowed for
Jonathan Kinsman, candidate for Mayor of the City of Riverside, to retain a check
he received on May 26, 1989. This was prior to the filing of FPPC Form 501
and 502 on June 1, 1989. The check in question was deposited on the same date
that the forms were filed. Thank you for giving this matter prompt consideration.


Sincerely,

Denise Stadelbacher

Denise Stadelbacher, Treasurer
Jonathan Kinsman for Mayor Campaign
Committee, ID# 890962

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 4, 1989


Denise Stadelbacher, Treasurer
Jonathan Kinsman for Mayor Campaign
Committee
4272½ University Avenue
Riverside, CA  92501

Re:  Letter No. 89-455

Dear Mr. Stadelbacher:

We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on July 28, 1989.  Your letter has
been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
for response.  If you have any questions, you may contact that
division directly at (916) 322-5662.

If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response.  A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process.  If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you.  In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

sections.conclusion: Mr. Kinsman may retain a contribution received prior to filing a Candidate Intention (Form 501) as required by Section 85200. There is no requirement in the Act or Commission regulations for returning contributions which are not in compliance with Section 85200.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a mayoral candidate may retain a campaign contribution received before filing the required Candidate Intention Form (Form 501) with the FPPC.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The candidate may retain the contribution received before filing Form 501, and there is no requirement under the Political Reform Act to return contributions that do not comply with Section 85200.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is the FPPC's written confirmation (dated August 29, 1989) of prior telephonic advice. The document also includes the incoming request letter from July 26, 1989 and an acknowledgment letter from August 4, 1989. OCR quality is generally good. Minor name variation in recipient ('Stadelbaeher' vs 'Stadelbacher') appears to be OCR error.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May Jonathan Kinsman, candidate for Mayor of the City of Riverside, retain a check he received on May 26, 1989, prior to the filing of FPPC Form 501 and 502 on June 1, 1989, with the check deposited on the same date that the forms were filed?

CONCLUSION: Mr. Kinsman may retain a contribution received prior to filing a Candidate Intention (Form 501) as required by Section 85200. There is no requirement in the Act or Commission regulations for returning contributions which are not in compliance with Section 85200.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirmed telephonic advice to a mayoral campaign treasurer that the candidate may retain a campaign contribution received before filing the required Candidate Intention Form, with no statutory requirement to return non-compliant contributions.
